lessons: Zod .default() fills partials — use .optional() + ?? in action body
app schema RPCs not in generated types — narrow-cast the rpc call
production_status REVOKE means schema must explicitly exclude it from update partials AND defense-in-depth strip at runtime
server-only package throws on import — vitest needs an alias to a no-op stub; Next.js still resolves the real one via react-server export condition
Schema column names matter — workstations.last_activity_at not last_heartbeat_at

rarv_cycles.scope_notes: PIN + record_scan_event wait for Phase 3 migrations

lessons: shop_settings lock invariant tested explicitly — timezone/currency/job_number_prefix cannot change after first job
supabase.rpc(name) defaults to public schema regardless of where the function actually lives — must use .schema('app').rpc() AND expose 'app' in supabase/config.toml [api].schemas

auth.admin.inviteUserByEmail does NOT accept app_metadata. Use createUser({app_metadata: {...}}) then generateLink({type: 'invite'}). The link_auth_user_to_actor trigger requires app_metadata.tenant_id at INSERT time on auth.users — without it the trigger raises auth_user_created_without_tenant_id.
PostgREST .or() with template-string concatenation is filter-injection prone. encodeURIComponent does NOT escape parens (RFC 3986 unreserved) — use explicit char map for ,():

rarv_cycles.scope_notes: Playwright E2E (Chromium) — list/create/detail/edit/packet lifecycle in describe.serial; auth fixture with E2E_STAFF_EMAIL/PASSWORD env vars
lessons: vitest exclude override loses defaults — must spread configDefaults.exclude before adding tests/e2e/** or zod's own tests in node_modules get collected (saw 2039-test run before fix)
Office sign-in fixture: page.waitForURL('**/dashboard') is the right success signal — sign-in action redirects to '/' which the office shell forwards to '/dashboard' via requireOfficeStaff

Brief-A 'safe parallel' files: tests/e2e/**, playwright.config.ts, vitest.config.ts (additive only), .gitignore (additive only) — no src/** writes keeps the brief composable with companies/portal briefs running concurrently

patterns: Server Action template: 'use server' → Zod safeParse → require* guard → getCurrentClaims (if needed) → createClient → typed insert/update → logAuditEvent → return narrow row
Query template: import 'server-only' → Zod parse → require* guard → createClient → chained .from().select().eq().order().range() → throw on .error → return data ?? []
Test template: vi.mock the four shared deps (db/server, db/admin, auth-helpers, audit) → typed chain mock or makeChain helper for awaitable builders → use RFC4122 v4 UUIDs in fixtures
Module index.ts re-exports actions + queries + their input/output types

RPC routing template: createClient → (supabase as unknown as {schema: ...}).schema('app') → .rpc<TResult, TArgs>(fn, args). Generic type defaults TArgs=void must be specified explicitly when passing args.

Staff invite template: insert app row → admin.createUser({app_metadata: {tenant_id, intended_actor}}) → admin.generateLink({type: 'invite'}) → roll back app row only if createUser fails; never roll back if generateLink fails (auth user already exists).
User-input search template: Zod-validate q (max length) → escapeForOr(q) before embedding in .or() template strings
anti_patterns_caught: Mocked supabase.rpc/admin tests pass even when the real call path is wrong — assert the mockSchema('app') call shape AND the createUser app_metadata shape, not just that the wrapper returns the expected fixture.
Zod .default() inside a schema that's later .partial()'d for update inputs will silently include the default in patch payloads. Use .optional() and apply default in the action body.
Re-using a module's index.ts edit pattern without Read first — old comment text in scaffolds varies subtly (ATTACH/APPLY vs INVITE/CREATE etc.).
